items.title: Updated: Press dinner shooting in D.C.: What we know so far
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>A man fired gunshots during a press dinner at the White House. While President Trump and Vice-President Vance were rushed to safety, the alleged perpetrator was overpowered by security agents. One agent was wounded but is not in critical condition.

Three DW journalists who attended the event, Ines Pohl, Janelle Dumalaon and Misha Komadovsky, share their experience. DW also spoke to terrorism expert Hans-Jakob Schindler, asking him about security incidents involving the US president. 

We also included an interview with Bruce Hoffman, a Senior Fellow for Counterterrorism and Homeland Security at the Council on Foreign Relations. Mr. Hoffman is the author of “God, Guns, and Sedition: Far-Right Terrorism in America.”

#whitehouse #dinner #shooting #trump #dwcurrentaffairs 

Chapters: 
0:00 Gunman opens fire at gala dinner attended by Trump 
0:33 Report by David Shaw 
1:03 Janelle Dumalaon’s eyewitness account 
2:32 Ines Pohl reporting from outside the Washington Hilton hotel 
3:07 Images of the alleged perpetrator 
3:22 Trump: ‘They seem to think he’s a lone wolf’ 
4:14 Misha Komadovsky’s eyewitness account 
5:46 ‘It was a mix of confusion and adrenaline’ 
7:30 The wounded agent’s injuries are not life-threatening 
8:04 What is known about the gunman? 
9:03 Trump: ‘Its a dangerous profession, but I’m here to do a job’ 
12:22 Interview Hans Jakob Schindler 
13:17 ‘The security system actually worked’ 
14:04 ‘There has been an uptick of political violence’ in the US and in Europe 
17:05 ‘The lone actor phenomenon is just about the hardest one to counter for security agencies’ 
18:37  Interview Bruce Hoffman
21:10  ‘We see a country that’s very divided, that’s very fractured, that has ready access to firearms, where many people have mental health issues’
23:03  ‘There’s rhetoric in the aftermath of these tragedies or near-tragedies that attempt to defuse the fervor that one sees in support of political violence’
24:39 Social media also fuel divisiveness, anger, hatred


For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: The mayor’s psychologist | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Leading a city as a mayor in Germany means carrying constant pressure, and they sometimes experience aggression. Psychologist Mercedes Mende works with mayors around Germany. She believes seeking mental health support is necessary for good leadership — and democracy itself.

items.title: How did a shooter get so close to Trump again?
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>A gunman stormed the lobby of the hotel where President Trump, top US officials and journalists were attending the annual White House Correspondents' Association dinner in Washington. The suspect fired a shotgun at a Secret Service agent before being tackled and arrested, and will be charged in federal court on Monday, authorities said. Meanwhile many wonder how an attacker could get so close to President Trump once more. 

#trump #dinner #whitehouse #dwnewsshorts

items.title: Gazans mourn after weekend of Israeli attacks
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Nearly a dozen Palestinians in Gaza have been killed in a series of Israeli attacks over the weekend, according to Gazan medical officials. The bodies of two men killed in an Israeli drone strike were taken to al-Shifa hospital, while Nassar hospital confirmed receiving the body of a woman shot by Israeli gunfire. The Israeli military says it is looking into the reports.  

#gazawar #gaza #israel #dwnewsshorts

items.title: Trump administrations brings back death by shooting
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>The Trump administration plans to add firing squads as a methods for executing people convicted of some federal crimes. The US Department of Justice recommended expanding its range beyond lethal injections, amid a shortage of access to deadly chemicals. Death sentences are also to be carried out more quickly. The new methods in federal prisons will include electrocution, gas asphyxiation and shooting.

#deathpenaltydebate #execution #firingsquad #lethalinjection #Trump #justice #dwnews

Chapters: 
00:00 Intro
00:41 Chart: Execution methods in the US
01:02 Why is the White House tightening the death penalty?
01:26 Interview with journalist Erik Kirschbaum
01:35 Firing squads amid a shortage of lethal chemicals
02:20 Which crimes are punishable by death?
02:55 Why four states account for more than 70% of US executions
04:31 Could innocent people be executed?
05:01 Dozens of murder convictions overturned through DNA evidence
05:30 Do people still witness executions?
06:40 Ethics debate: How cruel are executions?
09:22 Chart: Executions worldwide
10:05 Interview with Carolin Hoyle, Professor of Criminology, Oxford University
11:38 Prisons struggle to find doctors for executions
12:49 Firing squads: Does the US stand out internationally?
13:42 Death penalty: Does it deter crime?
15:43 Why is the number of executions rising?
18:09 Second part of the interview with journalist Erik Kirschbaum
18:29 Trump statement on the death penalty
18:45 Death penalty for migrants in the US
20:14 Executions as a political weapon?
20:45 Federal agents killing migrants during operations: what is known?
21:50 Does Trump benefit politically?
22:55 Another Trump statement on the death penalty
23:45 How safe are people in the United States?
25:40 Is there really a shortage of lethal chemicals?
27:15 Statement by Pope Leo on the death penalty
28:05 What is your view on the death penalty?

items.title: How Baltic volunteers are preparing for a conflict with Russia | Focus on europe
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Thousands of men and women are training all over Estonia, Latvia, and Lithuania. They prepare for a conflict with Russia they hope to avoid. 

Weekend combat drills in the Baltic states show how civilian volunteers prepare for war amid Russia's war against Ukraine. In Latvia and Lithuania, medics and fighters train with real weapons and lifelike simulations — from battlefield first aid in abandoned Soviet buildings to 360‑degree defense in frozen forests. Latvia’s Zemessardze, Lithuania's Riflemen's Union and Estonia's Kaitseliit count tens of thousands. With Russia next door on NATO's eastern border, volunteers say preparedness and patriotism are essential. 

#dwcurrentaffairs #nato #baltics #russia 

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: How could a gunman get so close to Trump again? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>US President Donald Trump is unharmed after a shooting during a prestigious annual press dinner in Washington, D.C. It’s the third time since 2024 that Trump has come under threat by an attacker in his vicinity. DW’s Ben Fajzullin and guests break down the latest, and the question: How could this happen again?

items.title: How Chernobyl still affects us | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Chernobyl is still running one of the largest unintentional experiments in scientific history, forty years after the explosion,. Here's what's still happening. Radioactive boar meat across Europe. Dogs evolving in the exclusion zone. A reactor fungus being tested for Mars. It’s effectively an experiment that never stopped.

#dwscience  #chernobylanniversary #nuclearscience
 

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: DW reporter heard gunshots at Trump dinner | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>DW correspondent Janelle Dumalaon heard several gunshots during an armed attack on a dinner that US President Donald Trump was attending. She sheltered in a bathroom as the incident unfolded. Trump was on stage in the ballroom as an armed man attempted to storm past security in the lobby. Authorities said he fired several shots before being arrested.

#dwnews #trump #shooting #usa

items.title: The Strait of Malacca: a small route with a massive impact | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>As oil prices react to turmoil in the Strait of Hormuz, another critical chokepoint is coming into focus: the Strait of Malacca. It carries the energy needs of China and its neighbors — and any disruption could ripple across the region and trigger severe consequences for the global economy. 

#DWGeopolitics
